C[illluck: I think Q14 here is the move. This way R17 aji remains.
illluck: Note how if black plays Q14 white will find the R14 stone hard to move. And if white plays R17 black can block at Q17 and shut white inside the corner.
C[illluck: I think black should just either P15 or O16 here to move out (I prefer P15 myself).
C[illluck: I still feel like black should come out at P15 in order to split white into two groups. Getting shut inside the corner like this is rarely a good result.
C[illluck: D6 and E5 are more common here - the idea is split white into two groups and it will be hard for white to take care of both at the same time.
C[illluck: I think this a nice idea - securing corner first and aim to cut at G4.
C[illluck: I think black should cut at G4 here.
C[illluck: This shape still has a weakness at C17 - C14 or C15 are normal here. The idea here (connection to D10, I presume) is still reasonable, though.
C[illluck: B14 is the common sequence here.
illluck: Sorry, this should be for move 40.
C[illluck: Good move - I think white should have played this point first.
C[illluck: I think black can L4 and then N4 first. N5 helps white fix the weakness.
C[illluck: Black can protect this cut a bit more actively (M6, for example) - letting white get L7 atari is painful.
C[illluck: This is a bit too ambitious, F8 is fine.

C[illluck: I think black is still has a won game here - white made up the handicap stones, but only about half of the reverse komi - it doesn't seem like there are any major areas left for white to get enough.
Thank you for the game, quite interesting.
